Show in this document

📘 traveling-salesman RuleSpeak

Traveling Salesman research domain at city/neighborhood/address scale. The rulebook normalizes finite weighted city graphs, validates explicit ordered-tour witnesses, records typed frontier obligations, derives a degree-two lower bound, and certifies the supplied Gridville route as optimal for its declared finite instance without claiming a general solver.

Declarative business rules rendered from the rulebook — the single source of truth. Every statement below is the plain-language reading of a formula; nothing here is hand-authored prose.

Use the in the top-right corner to turn each part of the documentation on or off — RuleSpeak, the literal reading, the raw formula, the author's description, and the links to a rule's inputs and consumers. Your choices are remembered.

City

Cities provide the outer spatial boundary for TSP instances.

In RuleSpeak

A city's name is the same as its display name.

In plain words

Display name.

As a formula
= Display Name
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Display NameCity Human-readable city name.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
↗ City Name Neighborhood

Σ Count of Stops Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-8
In RuleSpeak

A TSP instance's count of stops is the number of instance stops related to the TSP instance.

In plain words

Count of instance stops whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(TSP Instance, TSP Instance ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢TSP InstanceInstance Stop TSP instance containing the stop. ●TSP Instance IDTSP Instance Stable TSP instance identifier.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Expected Undirected Edge Count TSP Instance Is Complete Undirected Graph TSP Instance Actual Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Graph Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance

Number of normalized instance-stop rows.

Σ Count of Required Stops Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-9
In RuleSpeak

A TSP instance's count of required stops is the number of the TSP instance's instance stops that are required.

In plain words

Count of instance stops whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID and is required is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(TSP Instance, TSP Instance ID, Is Required, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢TSP InstanceInstance Stop TSP instance containing the stop. ●TSP Instance IDTSP Instance Stable TSP instance identifier. ●Is RequiredInstance Stop Whether the tour must visit this stop exactly once.

Σ Count of Non Unique Edge Pair Rows Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-12
In RuleSpeak

A TSP instance's count of non unique edge pair rows is the number of the TSP instance's travel edges that are not unique canonical pairs.

In plain words

Count of travel edges whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID and is unique canonical pair is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(TSP Instance, TSP Instance ID, Is Unique Canonical Pair, FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢TSP InstanceTravel Edge TSP instance owning the edge. ●TSP Instance IDTSP Instance Stable TSP instance identifier. ƒIs Unique Canonical PairTravel Edge Pair multiplicity equals 1.
Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one

⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge

Rows participating in a duplicated canonical unordered edge pair.

ƒ Expected Undirected Edge Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-13
In RuleSpeak

A TSP instance's expected undirected edge count is computed as the count of stops times the count of stops minus 1 divided by 2.

In plain words

(count of stops times (count of stops minus 1)) divided by 2.

As a formula
= (Count of Stops * (Count of Stops - 1)) / 2
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣCount of StopsTSP Instance Count of instance stops whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Complete Undirected Graph TSP Instance Actual Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Graph Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance

n(n-1)/2 expected edges for a complete undirected graph.

ƒ Is Complete Undirected Graph Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-14
In RuleSpeak

A TSP instance is considered a complete undirected graph if all of the following:

    • the count of travel edges is the expected undirected edge count
    • the count of inadmissible edges is 0
    • the count of non unique edge pair rows is 0
In plain words

Count of travel edges equals expected undirected edge count, count of inadmissible edges equals 0, and count of non unique edge pair rows equals 0.

As a formula
= AND(Count of Travel Edges = Expected Undirected Edge Count, Count of Inadmissible Edges = 0, Count of Non Unique Edge Pair Rows = 0)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ΣCount of Travel EdgesTSP Instance Count of travel edges whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID. ƒExpected Undirected Edge CountTSP Instance (count of stops times (count of stops minus 1)) divided by 2. ΣCount of Inadmissible EdgesTSP Instance Count of travel edges whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID and is admissible is false(). ΣCount of Non Unique Edge Pair RowsTSP Instance Count of travel edges whose TSP instance is TSP instance ID and is unique canonical pair is false().
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Actual Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Graph Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● Is Available Travel Edge ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge

Whether the instance has the expected edge count, no inadmissible rows, and no duplicated unordered pair rows.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-19
In RuleSpeak

A TSP graph invariant check is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the expected complete undirected graph is the actual complete undirected graph
    • the expected travel edge count is the actual travel edge count
    • the expected non unique edge pair rows is the actual non unique edge pair rows
In plain words

Expected complete undirected graph equals actual complete undirected graph, expected travel edge count equals actual travel edge count, and expected non unique edge pair rows equals actual non unique edge pair rows.

As a formula
= AND(Expected Complete Undirected Graph = Actual Complete Undirected Graph, Expected Travel Edge Count = Actual Travel Edge Count, Expected Non Unique Edge Pair Rows = Actual Non Unique Edge Pair Rows)
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
●Expected Complete Undirected GraphTSP Graph Invariant Check Expected graph-completeness status. ↗Actual Complete Undirected GraphTSP Graph Invariant Check The is complete undirected graph of the linked TSP instance. ●Expected Travel Edge CountTSP Graph Invariant Check Expected raw edge-row count. ↗Actual Travel Edge CountTSP Graph Invariant Check The count of travel edges of the linked TSP instance. ●Expected Non Unique Edge Pair RowsTSP Graph Invariant Check Expected number of rows participating in duplicated pair keys. ↗Actual Non Unique Edge Pair RowsTSP Graph Invariant Check The count of non unique edge pair rows of the linked TSP instance.
Ground truth at the leaves 12 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expected Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● Is Available Travel Edge ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance TSP Graph Invariant Check ● Expected Travel Edge Count TSP Graph Invariant Check ● Expected Non Unique Edge Pair Rows TSP Graph Invariant Check

Whether all graph expectations agree with derived values.

↗ Instance City Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-20
In RuleSpeak

The city of the linked TSP instance.

As a formula
= INDEX(City, MATCH(TSP Instance, TSP Instance ID, 0))

ƒ Is Within Instance City Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-27
In RuleSpeak

An instance stop is considered a within instance city if the address city is the instance city.

In plain words

Address city equals instance city.

As a formula
= Address City = Instance City
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Address CityInstance Stop The city of the linked address. ↗Instance CityInstance Stop The city of the linked TSP instance.
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ City Neighborhood ⇢ Neighborhood Address ● Neighborhood ID Neighborhood ⇢ Address Instance Stop ● Address ID Address ⇢ City TSP Instance ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance

Whether the selected address belongs to the instance city.

ƒ Is Depot Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-28
In RuleSpeak

An instance stop is considered a depot if the address is the instance depot address.

In plain words

Address equals instance depot address.

As a formula
= Address = Instance Depot Address
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢AddressInstance Stop Address selected into the instance. ↗Instance Depot AddressInstance Stop The depot address of the linked TSP instance.

ƒ Count of Incident Edges Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-31
In RuleSpeak

Count of canonical edges from plus count of canonical edges to.

As a formula

Canonical unordered endpoint identity supplied as data and checked against the two endpoint rows.

ƒ Expected Canonical Pair Key Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-35
In RuleSpeak

The travel edge's expected canonical pair key is determined by the following priority:

  1. the from stop, followed by “|”, followed by the to stop if the from stop is less than the to stop
  2. the to stop, followed by “|”, followed by the from stop in all other cases
In plain words

If from stop is less than to stop then from stop, "|", joined with to stop, otherwise to stop, "|", joined with from stop.

As a formula
= IF(From Stop < To Stop, CONCAT(From Stop, "|", To Stop), CONCAT(To Stop, "|", From Stop))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢From StopTravel Edge First canonical endpoint. ⇢To StopTravel Edge Second canonical endpoint.
Consumers 1 direct +40 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Canonical Pair Key Correct Travel Edge Is Admissible Travel Edge Count of Inadmissible Edges TSP Instance Count of Admissible Edges From Instance Stop Count of Admissible Edges to Instance Stop Edge is Admissible Tour Leg Is Complete Undirected Graph TSP Instance Count of Admissible Incident Edges Instance Stop Is Valid Tour Leg Actual Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check Stop Admissible Incident Edge Count Local Degree Bound Count of Invalid Legs Candidate Tour Travel Cost Tour Leg Is Passing TSP Graph Invariant Check Required Dominance Check Count Local Degree Bound Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Candidate Tour Total Travel Cost Candidate Tour Is Two Cheapest Witness Local Degree Bound Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Candidate is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Optimality Certificate Actual Hamiltonian Cycle Witness TSP Invariant Check Candidate Travel Cost Optimality Certificate Actual Total Travel Cost TSP Invariant Check Local Bound Cost Local Degree Bound Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Passing Optimality Certificate Is Hamiltonian Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Cost Correct TSP Invariant Check Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Certified Instance Lower Bound Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Passing TSP Invariant Check Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound is Certified Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate

⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge

Endpoint-derived canonical pair key used to verify the supplied pair identity.

ƒ Is Canonical Pair Key Correct Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-36
In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered a canonical pair key correct if the canonical pair key is the expected canonical pair key.

In plain words

Canonical pair key equals expected canonical pair key.

As a formula
= Canonical Pair Key = Expected Canonical Pair Key
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Canonical Pair KeyTravel Edge Canonical unordered endpoint identity supplied as data and checked against the two endpoint rows. ƒExpected Canonical Pair KeyTravel Edge If from stop is less than to stop then from stop, "|", joined with to stop, otherwise to stop, "|", joined with from stop.

In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered a canonical endpoint order if the from stop is less than the to stop.

From stop is less than to stop.

As a formula
= From Stop < To Stop

ƒ Is Unique Canonical Pair Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-39
In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered a unique canonical pair if the pair multiplicity is 1.

In plain words

Pair multiplicity equals 1.

As a formula
= Pair Multiplicity = 1

ƒ Is Self Loop Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-42
In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered a self loop if the from stop is the to stop.

In plain words

From stop equals to stop.

As a formula
= From Stop = To Stop
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢From StopTravel Edge First canonical endpoint. ⇢To StopTravel Edge Second canonical endpoint.

Whether both endpoints are the same stop.

ƒ Is Within Instance Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-43
In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered a within instance if all of the following:

    • the TSP instance is the from TSP instance
    • the TSP instance is the to TSP instance
In plain words

TSP instance equals from TSP instance and TSP instance equals to TSP instance.

As a formula
= AND(TSP Instance = From TSP Instance, TSP Instance = To TSP Instance)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢TSP InstanceTravel Edge TSP instance owning the edge. ↗From TSP InstanceTravel Edge The TSP instance of the linked from stop. ↗To TSP InstanceTravel Edge The TSP instance of the linked to stop.

Whether the edge and both endpoints belong to one instance.

ƒ Is Admissible Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-44
In RuleSpeak

A travel edge is considered admissible if all of the following:

    • the available flag is set
    • the self loop flag is not set
    • the within instance flag is set
    • the canonical endpoint order flag is set
    • the canonical pair key correct flag is set
    • the unique canonical pair flag is set
In plain words

Is available, not is self loop, is within instance, is canonical endpoint order, is canonical pair key correct, and is unique canonical pair.

As a formula
= AND(Is Available, NOT(Is Self Loop), Is Within Instance, Is Canonical Endpoint Order, Is Canonical Pair Key Correct, Is Unique Canonical Pair)
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
●Is AvailableTravel Edge Whether the edge remains available after current normalization. ƒIs Self LoopTravel Edge From stop equals to stop. ƒIs Within InstanceTravel Edge TSP instance equals from TSP instance and TSP instance equals to TSP instance. ƒIs Canonical Endpoint OrderTravel Edge From stop is less than to stop. ƒIs Canonical Pair Key CorrectTravel Edge Canonical pair key equals expected canonical pair key. ƒIs Unique Canonical PairTravel Edge Pair multiplicity equals 1.
Consumers 4 direct +35 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Count of Inadmissible Edges TSP Instance Σ Count of Admissible Edges From Instance Stop Σ Count of Admissible Edges to Instance Stop ↗ Edge is Admissible Tour Leg Is Complete Undirected Graph TSP Instance Count of Admissible Incident Edges Instance Stop Is Valid Tour Leg Actual Complete Undirected Graph TSP Graph Invariant Check Stop Admissible Incident Edge Count Local Degree Bound Count of Invalid Legs Candidate Tour Travel Cost Tour Leg Is Passing TSP Graph Invariant Check Required Dominance Check Count Local Degree Bound Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Candidate Tour Total Travel Cost Candidate Tour Is Two Cheapest Witness Local Degree Bound Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Candidate is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Optimality Certificate Actual Hamiltonian Cycle Witness TSP Invariant Check Candidate Travel Cost Optimality Certificate Actual Total Travel Cost TSP Invariant Check Local Bound Cost Local Degree Bound Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Passing Optimality Certificate Is Hamiltonian Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Cost Correct TSP Invariant Check Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Certified Instance Lower Bound Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Passing TSP Invariant Check Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound is Certified Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate

First-order edge admissibility including availability, instance membership, canonical endpoint order, correct pair identity, and pair uniqueness.

Candidate Tour

Explicit candidate tours. Validity is derived; optimality is deliberately not inferred.

Display name.

As a formula
= Display Name

●Display NameCandidate Tour Human-readable candidate-tour name.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable candidate-tour name.

⇢ TSP Instance Relationship string

A pointer to TSP Instance. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Backtracking performed to obtain this candidate.

In RuleSpeak

ƒ Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-59
In RuleSpeak

A candidate tour is considered a hamiltonian cycle witness if all of the following:

    • the count of tour stops is the required stop count
    • the count of tour legs is the required stop count
    • the count of non unique visits is 0
    • the count of non unique sequences is 0
    • the count of out of instance stops is 0
    • the count of non required stops is 0
    • the count of depot stops is 1
    • the count of depot position violations is 0
    • the count of invalid legs is 0
    • the count of cycle degree violations is 0
    • the count of non unique leg orders is 0
In plain words

Count of tour stops equals required stop count, count of tour legs equals required stop count, count of non unique visits equals 0, count of non unique sequences equals 0, count of out of instance stops equals 0, count of non required stops equals 0, count of depot stops equals 1, count of depot position violations equals 0, count of invalid legs equals 0, count of cycle degree violations equals 0, and count of non unique leg orders equals 0.

As a formula
= AND(Count of Tour Stops = Required Stop Count, Count of Tour Legs = Required Stop Count, Count of Non Unique Visits = 0, Count of Non Unique Sequences = 0, Count of Out of Instance Stops = 0, Count of Non Required Stops = 0, Count of Depot Stops = 1, Count of Depot Position Violations = 0, Count of Invalid Legs = 0, Count of Cycle Degree Violations = 0, Count of Non Unique Leg Orders = 0)
Inputs 12 fields this rule reads
ΣCount of Tour StopsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID. ↗Required Stop CountCandidate Tour The count of required stops of the linked TSP instance. ΣCount of Tour LegsCandidate Tour Count of tour legs whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID. ΣCount of Non Unique VisitsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is unique visit is false(). ΣCount of Non Unique SequencesCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is unique sequence is false(). ΣCount of Out of Instance StopsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is in tour instance is false(). ΣCount of Non Required StopsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and stop is required is false(). ΣCount of Depot StopsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and stop is depot is true(). ΣCount of Depot Position ViolationsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is depot at first position is false(). ΣCount of Invalid LegsCandidate Tour Count of tour legs whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is valid is false(). ΣCount of Cycle Degree ViolationsCandidate Tour Count of tour stops whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is cycle degree satisfied is false(). ΣCount of Non Unique Leg OrdersCandidate Tour Count of tour legs whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is unique leg order is false().
Consumers 4 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one

Ground truth at the leaves 23 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Whether the supplied ordered rows form one globally covered Hamiltonian cycle for the declared instance.

As a formula

ƒ Is Optimality Proved Calculated 17 hops from ground truth boolean DR-61
In RuleSpeak

A candidate tour is considered optimality-proved if all of the following:

    • the hamiltonian cycle witness flag is set
    • the count of passing optimality certificates is greater than 0
In plain words

Is hamiltonian cycle witness and count of passing optimality certificates is greater than 0.

As a formula
= AND(Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness, Count of Passing Optimality Certificates > 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessCandidate Tour Count of tour stops equals required stop count, count of tour legs equals required stop count, count of non unique visits equals 0, count of non unique sequences equals 0, count of out of instance stops equals 0, count of non required stops equals 0, count of depot stops equals 1, count of depot position violations equals 0, count of invalid legs equals 0, count of cycle degree violations equals 0, and count of non unique leg orders equals 0. ΣCount of Passing Optimality CertificatesCandidate Tour Count of optimality certificates whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID and is passing is true().
Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one

Ground truth at the leaves 40 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ Address Instance Stop ⇢ Depot Address TSP Instance ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Optimality Certificate ⇢ TSP Instance Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ⇢ Bound Certificate TSP Bound Term ● Instance Lower Bound ID Instance Lower Bound ● Counts Toward Adjustment TSP Bound Term ● Is Certified TSP Bound Term ● Required Supplemental Term Count Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Lower Bound Optimality Certificate ● Supplemental Bound Adjustment Instance Lower Bound

Whether at least one passing finite-instance optimality certificate closes this candidate's scope.

ƒ Residual Claim Calculated 18 hops from ground truth string DR-62
In RuleSpeak

The candidate tour's residual claim is determined by the following priority:

  1. “OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE” if the optimality proved flag is set
  2. “VALID_TOUR_NOT_OPTIMALITY_PROOF” if the hamiltonian cycle witness flag is set
  3. “INVALID_TOUR” in all other cases
In plain words

If is optimality proved then "OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE", otherwise if is hamiltonian cycle witness then "VALID_TOUR_NOT_OPTIMALITY_PROOF", otherwise "INVALID_TOUR".

As a formula
= IF(Is Optimality Proved, "OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE", IF(Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness, "VALID_TOUR_NOT_OPTIMALITY_PROOF", "INVALID_TOUR"))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Optimality ProvedCandidate Tour Is hamiltonian cycle witness and count of passing optimality certificates is greater than 0. ƒIs Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessCandidate Tour Count of tour stops equals required stop count, count of tour legs equals required stop count, count of non unique visits equals 0, count of non unique sequences equals 0, count of out of instance stops equals 0, count of non required stops equals 0, count of depot stops equals 1, count of depot position violations equals 0, count of invalid legs equals 0, count of cycle degree violations equals 0, and count of non unique leg orders equals 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 40 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Tour Stop

Ordered candidate-tour visits. Multiplicity and sequence uniqueness are derived from the row set.

A pointer to Candidate Tour. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

↗ Tour TSP Instance Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-63
In RuleSpeak

A tour stop's tour TSP instance — taken from the linked candidate tour.

In plain words

The TSP instance of the linked candidate tour.

As a formula
= INDEX(TSP Instance, MATCH(Candidate Tour, Candidate Tour ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads

ƒ Is Cycle Degree Satisfied Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-77
In RuleSpeak

A tour stop is considered cycle-degree-satisfied if all of the following:

    • the exactly one outgoing leg flag is set
    • the exactly one incoming leg flag is set
In plain words

Has exactly one outgoing leg and has exactly one incoming leg.

As a formula
= AND(Has Exactly One Outgoing Leg, Has Exactly One Incoming Leg)

In RuleSpeak

A tour leg's leg order multiplicity is the number of tour legs related to the tour leg.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Candidate Tour, Candidate Tour, Leg Order, Leg Order)

ƒ Is Unique Leg Order Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-92
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered a unique leg order if the leg order multiplicity is 1.

In plain words

Leg order multiplicity equals 1.

As a formula
= Leg Order Multiplicity = 1

ƒ Is Candidate Membership Valid Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-93
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered candidate-membership-valid if all of the following:

    • the candidate tour is the from tour candidate
    • the candidate tour is the to tour candidate
In plain words

Candidate tour equals from tour candidate and candidate tour equals to tour candidate.

As a formula
= AND(Candidate Tour = From Tour Candidate, Candidate Tour = To Tour Candidate)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢Candidate TourTour Leg Candidate tour containing the leg. ↗From Tour CandidateTour Leg The candidate tour of the linked from tour stop. ↗To Tour CandidateTour Leg The candidate tour of the linked to tour stop.

Whether both ordered visits belong to this candidate.

ƒ Is Sequence Transition Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-94
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered a sequence transition if all of the following:

    • the to sequence position is the from sequence position plus 1
    • all of these hold:
      • the from sequence position is the required stop count
      • the to sequence position is 1
In plain words

To sequence position equals (from sequence position plus 1) or from sequence position equals required stop count and to sequence position equals 1.

As a formula
= OR(To Sequence Position = From Sequence Position + 1, AND(From Sequence Position = Required Stop Count, To Sequence Position = 1))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
↗To Sequence PositionTour Leg The sequence position of the linked to tour stop. ↗From Sequence PositionTour Leg The sequence position of the linked from tour stop. ↗Required Stop CountTour Leg The count of required stops of the linked tour TSP instance.
Consumers 1 direct +19 transitive rules that read this one

Whether the leg follows the next visit or closes the route from n back to one.

ƒ Is Edge Endpoint Match Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-95
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered an edge endpoint match if all of the following:

    • all of these hold:
      • the from instance stop is the edge from stop
      • the to instance stop is the edge to stop
    • all of these hold:
      • the from instance stop is the edge to stop
      • the to instance stop is the edge from stop
In plain words

From instance stop equals edge from stop and to instance stop equals edge to stop or from instance stop equals edge to stop and to instance stop equals edge from stop.

As a formula
= OR(AND(From Instance Stop = Edge From Stop, To Instance Stop = Edge to Stop), AND(From Instance Stop = Edge to Stop, To Instance Stop = Edge From Stop))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
↗From Instance StopTour Leg The instance stop of the linked from tour stop. ↗Edge From StopTour Leg The from stop of the linked travel edge. ↗To Instance StopTour Leg The instance stop of the linked to tour stop. ↗Edge to StopTour Leg The to stop of the linked travel edge.

Whether the directed leg traverses the selected undirected edge in either direction.

ƒ Is in Tour Instance Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-96
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered in-tour-instance if the edge TSP instance is the tour TSP instance.

In plain words

Edge TSP instance equals tour TSP instance.

= Edge TSP Instance = Tour TSP Instance

Whether the edge belongs to the candidate's TSP instance.

ƒ Is Valid Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-97
In RuleSpeak

A tour leg is considered valid if all of the following:

    • the candidate membership valid flag is set
    • the sequence transition flag is set
    • the edge endpoint match flag is set
    • the in tour instance flag is set
    • the edge is admissible flag is set
    • the unique leg order flag is set
In plain words

Is candidate membership valid, is sequence transition, is edge endpoint match, is in tour instance, edge is admissible, and is unique leg order.

As a formula
= AND(Is Candidate Membership Valid, Is Sequence Transition, Is Edge Endpoint Match, Is in Tour Instance, Edge is Admissible, Is Unique Leg Order)
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Candidate Membership ValidTour Leg Candidate tour equals from tour candidate and candidate tour equals to tour candidate. ƒIs Sequence TransitionTour Leg To sequence position equals (from sequence position plus 1) or from sequence position equals required stop count and to sequence position equals 1. ƒIs Edge Endpoint MatchTour Leg From instance stop equals edge from stop and to instance stop equals edge to stop or from instance stop equals edge to stop and to instance stop equals edge from stop. ƒIs in Tour InstanceTour Leg Edge TSP instance equals tour TSP instance. ↗Edge is AdmissibleTour Leg The is admissible of the linked travel edge. ƒIs Unique Leg OrderTour Leg Leg order multiplicity equals 1.
Consumers 2 direct +17 transitive rules that read this one

⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg

Whether the leg passes candidate membership, sequence, endpoint, instance, admissibility, and unique-order checks.

ƒ Travel Cost Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-98
In RuleSpeak

The tour leg's travel cost is determined by the following priority:

  1. the edge travel cost if the valid flag is set
  2. 0 in all other cases
In plain words

If is valid then edge travel cost, otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Is Valid, Edge Travel Cost, 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ValidTour Leg Is candidate membership valid, is sequence transition, is edge endpoint match, is in tour instance, edge is admissible, and is unique leg order. ↗Edge Travel CostTour Leg The travel cost of the linked travel edge.

⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ● Travel Cost Travel Edge

Cost contributed by a valid leg; invalid legs contribute zero and are counted separately.

Local Degree Bound

One degree-two lower-bound witness per required stop. Each row names two incident edges and proves all other incident edges are no cheaper than the second selected edge.

⇢ TSP Instance Relationship string

A pointer to TSP Instance. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 4 direct +15 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Two Cheapest Witness Local Degree Bound Σ Count of Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Σ Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Σ Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Local Bound Cost Local Degree Bound Is Certified Instance Lower Bound Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound is Certified Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Passing Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

A pointer to Travel Edge. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

ƒ Is First Edge Incident Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-107
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound is considered a first edge incident if all of the following:

    • the instance stop is the first edge from stop
    • the instance stop is the first edge to stop
In plain words

Instance stop equals first edge from stop or instance stop equals first edge to stop.

As a formula
= OR(Instance Stop = First Edge From Stop, Instance Stop = First Edge to Stop)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢Instance StopLocal Degree Bound Required stop receiving the local degree-two bound. ↗First Edge From StopLocal Degree Bound The from stop of the linked first edge. ↗First Edge to StopLocal Degree Bound The to stop of the linked first edge.

ƒ Is Second Edge Incident Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-108
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound is considered a second edge incident if all of the following:

    • the instance stop is the second edge from stop
    • the instance stop is the second edge to stop
In plain words

Instance stop equals second edge from stop or instance stop equals second edge to stop.

As a formula
= OR(Instance Stop = Second Edge From Stop, Instance Stop = Second Edge to Stop)

⇢Instance StopLocal Degree Bound Required stop receiving the local degree-two bound. ↗Second Edge From StopLocal Degree Bound The from stop of the linked second edge. ↗Second Edge to StopLocal Degree Bound The to stop of the linked second edge.

ƒ Are Selected Edges Distinct Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-109
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound is considered a selected edges distinct if the first edge is not the second edge.

In plain words

First edge is not second edge.

As a formula
= First Edge <> Second Edge
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢First EdgeLocal Degree Bound Cheapest selected incident edge. ⇢Second EdgeLocal Degree Bound Second-cheapest selected incident edge.
Consumers 1 direct +17 transitive rules that read this one

⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound

Whether the two selected edge rows are distinct.

ƒ Are Selected Costs Ordered Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-110
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound is considered selected-costs-ordered if the first edge cost is at most the second edge cost.

In plain words

First edge cost is at most second edge cost.

As a formula
= First Edge Cost <= Second Edge Cost
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗First Edge CostLocal Degree Bound The travel cost of the linked first edge. ↗Second Edge CostLocal Degree Bound The travel cost of the linked second edge.

In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound's required dominance check count is computed as the stop admissible incident edge count minus 2.

In plain words

Stop admissible incident edge count minus 2.

As a formula
= Stop Admissible Incident Edge Count - 2

↗Stop Admissible Incident Edge CountLocal Degree Bound The count of admissible incident edges of the linked instance stop.

Number of other incident edges that must be compared against the second selected cost.

Σ Count of Dominance Checks Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-112
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound's count of dominance checks is the number of incident dominance checks related to the local degree bound.

In plain words

Count of incident dominance checks whose local degree bound is local degree bound ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Local Degree Bound, Local Degree Bound ID)

Represented comparisons against other incident edges.

Σ Count of Failed Dominance Checks Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth integer DR-113
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound's count of failed dominance checks is the number of the local degree bound's incident dominance checks that are not passing.

In plain words

Count of incident dominance checks whose local degree bound is local degree bound ID and is passing is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Local Degree Bound, Local Degree Bound ID, Is Passing, FALSE())

⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge

Dominance comparisons that fail incidence, distinctness, uniqueness, or cost ordering.

ƒ Is Two Cheapest Witness Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-114
In RuleSpeak

A local degree bound is considered a two cheapest witness if all of the following:

    • the TSP instance is the stop TSP instance
    • the first edge incident flag is set
    • the second edge incident flag is set
    • the are selected edges distinct flag is set
    • the are selected costs ordered flag is set
    • the count of dominance checks is the required dominance check count
    • the count of failed dominance checks is 0
In plain words

TSP instance equals stop TSP instance, is first edge incident, is second edge incident, are selected edges distinct, are selected costs ordered, count of dominance checks equals required dominance check count, and count of failed dominance checks equals 0.

As a formula
= AND(TSP Instance = Stop TSP Instance, Is First Edge Incident, Is Second Edge Incident, Are Selected Edges Distinct, Are Selected Costs Ordered, Count of Dominance Checks = Required Dominance Check Count, Count of Failed Dominance Checks = 0)
Inputs 9 fields this rule reads
⇢TSP InstanceLocal Degree Bound Instance whose tour degree requirement is being bounded. ↗Stop TSP InstanceLocal Degree Bound The TSP instance of the linked instance stop. ƒIs First Edge IncidentLocal Degree Bound Instance stop equals first edge from stop or instance stop equals first edge to stop. ƒIs Second Edge IncidentLocal Degree Bound Instance stop equals second edge from stop or instance stop equals second edge to stop. ƒAre Selected Edges DistinctLocal Degree Bound First edge is not second edge. ƒAre Selected Costs OrderedLocal Degree Bound First edge cost is at most second edge cost. ΣCount of Dominance ChecksLocal Degree Bound Count of incident dominance checks whose local degree bound is local degree bound ID. ƒRequired Dominance Check CountLocal Degree Bound Stop admissible incident edge count minus 2. ΣCount of Failed Dominance ChecksLocal Degree Bound Count of incident dominance checks whose local degree bound is local degree bound ID and is passing is false().
Consumers 2 direct +15 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Local Bound Cost Local Degree Bound Σ Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Certified Instance Lower Bound Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound is Certified Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Passing Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 16 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ TSP Instance Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check

Whether this row and its child comparisons certify the two cheapest admissible incident edges.

ƒ Local Bound Cost Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-115
In RuleSpeak

The local degree bound's local bound cost is determined by the following priority:

  1. the first edge cost plus the second edge cost if the two cheapest witness flag is set
  2. 0 in all other cases
In plain words

If is two cheapest witness then (first edge cost plus second edge cost), otherwise 0.

As a formula
= IF(Is Two Cheapest Witness, First Edge Cost + Second Edge Cost, 0)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Two Cheapest WitnessLocal Degree Bound TSP instance equals stop TSP instance, is first edge incident, is second edge incident, are selected edges distinct, are selected costs ordered, count of dominance checks equals required dominance check count, and count of failed dominance checks equals 0. ↗First Edge CostLocal Degree Bound The travel cost of the linked first edge. ↗Second Edge CostLocal Degree Bound The travel cost of the linked second edge.
Consumers 1 direct +12 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Passing Optimality Certificate Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 16 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ TSP Instance Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check

Certified sum of the two cheapest incident edge costs; zero when the witness is invalid.

Incident Dominance Check

Witness rows proving every unselected incident edge is at least as expensive as the second edge named by a LocalDegreeBound.

⇢ Local Degree Bound Relationship string

A pointer to Local Degree Bound. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 7 direct +23 transitive rules that read this one

Unselected incident edge compared against the second selected edge.

↗ Bound Stop Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-116
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check's bound stop is the instance stop of the incident dominance check's local degree bound.

In plain words

The instance stop of the linked local degree bound.

As a formula
= INDEX(Instance Stop, MATCH(Local Degree Bound, Local Degree Bound ID, 0))

Σ Other Edge Multiplicity Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-123
In RuleSpeak

In plain words

= COUNTIFS(Local Degree Bound, Local Degree Bound, Other Edge, Other Edge)

⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check

Number of comparison rows in the same local witness naming this other edge.

ƒ Is Other Edge Incident Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-124
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check is considered an other edge incident if all of the following:

    • the bound stop is the other edge from stop
    • the bound stop is the other edge to stop
In plain words

Bound stop equals other edge from stop or bound stop equals other edge to stop.

As a formula
= OR(Bound Stop = Other Edge From Stop, Bound Stop = Other Edge to Stop)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
↗Bound StopIncident Dominance Check The instance stop of the linked local degree bound. ↗Other Edge From StopIncident Dominance Check The from stop of the linked other edge. ↗Other Edge to StopIncident Dominance Check The to stop of the linked other edge.

Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Whether the compared edge is incident to the bounded stop.

ƒ Is Other Edge Unselected Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-125
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check is considered other-edge-unselected if all of the following:

    • the other edge is not the first selected edge
    • the other edge is not the second selected edge
In plain words

Other edge is not first selected edge and other edge is not second selected edge.

As a formula
= AND(Other Edge <> First Selected Edge, Other Edge <> Second Selected Edge)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢Other EdgeIncident Dominance Check Unselected incident edge compared against the second selected edge. ↗First Selected EdgeIncident Dominance Check The first edge of the linked local degree bound. ↗Second Selected EdgeIncident Dominance Check The second edge of the linked local degree bound.

Whether the compared edge differs from both selected edges.

ƒ Is Other Edge Unique Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-126
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check is considered an other edge unique if the other edge multiplicity is 1.

In plain words

Other edge multiplicity equals 1.

As a formula
= Other Edge Multiplicity = 1
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣOther Edge MultiplicityIncident Dominance Check Count of incident dominance checks whose local degree bound is local degree bound and other edge is other edge.
Consumers 1 direct +19 transitive rules that read this one

⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check

Whether this unselected edge appears exactly once among the parent's comparisons.

ƒ Is Dominated by Second Cost Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-127
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check is considered a dominated by second cost if the other edge cost is at least the second selected edge cost.

In plain words

Other edge cost is at least second selected edge cost.

As a formula
= Other Edge Cost >= Second Selected Edge Cost
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Other Edge CostIncident Dominance Check The travel cost of the linked other edge. ↗Second Selected Edge CostIncident Dominance Check The second edge cost of the linked local degree bound.
Consumers 1 direct +19 transitive rules that read this one

● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound

Whether the unselected edge is no cheaper than the second selected edge.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 4 hops from ground truth boolean DR-128
In RuleSpeak

An incident dominance check is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the other edge incident flag is set
    • the other edge unselected flag is set
    • the other edge unique flag is set
    • the dominated by second cost flag is set
In plain words

Is other edge incident, is other edge unselected, is other edge unique, and is dominated by second cost.

As a formula
= AND(Is Other Edge Incident, Is Other Edge Unselected, Is Other Edge Unique, Is Dominated by Second Cost)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Other Edge IncidentIncident Dominance Check Bound stop equals other edge from stop or bound stop equals other edge to stop. ƒIs Other Edge UnselectedIncident Dominance Check Other edge is not first selected edge and other edge is not second selected edge. ƒIs Other Edge UniqueIncident Dominance Check Other edge multiplicity equals 1. ƒIs Dominated by Second CostIncident Dominance Check Other edge cost is at least second selected edge cost.

⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge

Whether the comparison is incident, unselected, unique, and cost-dominated.

Instance Lower Bound

Global lower bounds obtained by summing certified local degree-two bounds and dividing by two because each tour edge is incident to two stops.

⇢ TSP Instance Relationship string

A pointer to TSP Instance. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 5 direct +15 transitive rules that read this one
↗ Required Stop Count Instance Lower Bound Σ Count of Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Σ Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Instance Lower Bound Σ Total Local Degree Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound ↗ Lower Bound TSP Instance Optimality Certificate Is Certified Instance Lower Bound Base Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Same Instance Optimality Certificate Lower Bound is Certified Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Instance Lower Bound Is Passing Optimality Certificate Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

Instance receiving the lower bound.

● Bound Kind Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Kind of reusable lower-bound geometry.

↗ Required Stop Count Lookup 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-129
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's required stop count is the count of required stops of the instance lower bound's TSP instance.

In plain words

The count of required stops of the linked TSP instance.

As a formula
= INDEX(Count of Required Stops, MATCH(TSP Instance, TSP Instance ID, 0))

Local degree-bound rows represented for the instance.

Σ Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-131
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's count of invalid local degree bounds is the number of the instance lower bound's local degree bounds that are not two cheapest witnesses.

In plain words

Count of local degree bounds whose TSP instance is TSP instance and is two cheapest witness is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(TSP Instance, TSP Instance, Is Two Cheapest Witness, FALSE())

Σ Total Local Degree Bound Cost Aggregation 10 hops from ground truth number DR-132
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's total local degree bound cost is the total local bound cost across the local degree bounds related to the instance lower bound.

In plain words

Sum of local bound cost across all local degree bounds whose TSP instance is TSP instance.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Local Bound Cost, TSP Instance, TSP Instance)

ƒLocal Bound CostLocal Degree Bound If is two cheapest witness then (first edge cost plus second edge cost), otherwise 0. ⇢TSP InstanceLocal Degree Bound Instance whose tour degree requirement is being bounded. ⇢TSP InstanceInstance Lower Bound Instance receiving the lower bound.

Sum of the local two-edge lower bounds across all required stops.

ƒ Lower Bound Cost Calculated 12 hops from ground truth number DR-133
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's lower bound cost is computed as the base lower bound cost plus the supplemental bound adjustment.

In plain words

Base lower bound cost plus supplemental bound adjustment.

As a formula
= Base Lower Bound Cost + Supplemental Bound Adjustment
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒBase Lower Bound CostInstance Lower Bound Total local degree bound cost divided by 2. ●Supplemental Bound AdjustmentInstance Lower Bound Signed sum of certified repair terms.

↗ Lower Bound Cost Optimality Certificate Is Bound Tight Optimality Certificate Is Passing Optimality Certificate Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

Certified global tour lower bound after double-count correction.

ƒ Is Certified Calculated 10 hops from ground truth boolean DR-134
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound is considered certified if all of the following:

    • the count of local degree bounds is the required stop count
    • the count of invalid local degree bounds is 0
    • the supplemental bound certified flag is set
In plain words

Count of local degree bounds equals required stop count, count of invalid local degree bounds equals 0, and is supplemental bound certified.

As a formula
= AND(Count of Local Degree Bounds = Required Stop Count, Count of Invalid Local Degree Bounds = 0, Is Supplemental Bound Certified)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ΣCount of Local Degree BoundsInstance Lower Bound Count of local degree bounds whose TSP instance is TSP instance. ↗Required Stop CountInstance Lower Bound The count of required stops of the linked TSP instance. ΣCount of Invalid Local Degree BoundsInstance Lower Bound Count of local degree bounds whose TSP instance is TSP instance and is two cheapest witness is false(). ƒIs Supplemental Bound CertifiedInstance Lower Bound Count of certified supplemental terms equals required supplemental term count.
Consumers 1 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one

Whether every required stop has one valid local witness and the global double-counting aggregation is closed.

ƒ Base Lower Bound Cost Calculated 11 hops from ground truth number DR-135
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's base lower bound cost is computed as the total local degree bound cost divided by 2.

In plain words

Total local degree bound cost divided by 2.

As a formula
= Total Local Degree Bound Cost / 2
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Local Degree Bound CostInstance Lower Bound Sum of local bound cost across all local degree bounds whose TSP instance is TSP instance.

Σ Count of Certified Supplemental Terms Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-136
In RuleSpeak

An instance lower bound's count of certified supplemental terms is the number of the instance lower bound's TSP bound terms that count a toward adjustment and are certified.

In plain words

Count of TSP bound terms whose bound certificate is instance lower bound ID, counts toward adjustment is true(), and is certified is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Bound Certificate, Instance Lower Bound ID, Counts Toward Adjustment, TRUE(), Is Certified, TRUE())

ƒ Term Value Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-138

Witnessed reason for the term.

Optimality Certificate

Finite-instance optimality certificates. Passing requires a valid Hamiltonian witness, a certified lower bound for the same instance, and equality between candidate cost and lower bound.

⇢ Candidate Tour Relationship string

A pointer to Candidate Tour. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

ƒ Is Same Instance Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-145
In RuleSpeak

An optimality certificate is considered a same instance if the candidate TSP instance is the lower bound TSP instance.

Candidate TSP instance equals lower bound TSP instance.

= Candidate TSP Instance = Lower Bound TSP Instance

ƒ Is Bound Tight Calculated 14 hops from ground truth boolean DR-146
In RuleSpeak

An optimality certificate is considered a bound tight if the candidate travel cost is the lower bound cost.

In plain words

Candidate travel cost equals lower bound cost.

= Candidate Travel Cost = Lower Bound Cost

Whether the candidate cost equals the certified lower bound.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 15 hops from ground truth boolean DR-147
In RuleSpeak

An optimality certificate is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the candidate is hamiltonian cycle witness flag is set
    • the lower bound is certified flag is set
    • the same instance flag is set
    • the bound tight flag is set
In plain words

Candidate is hamiltonian cycle witness, lower bound is certified, is same instance, and is bound tight.

As a formula
= AND(Candidate is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness, Lower Bound is Certified, Is Same Instance, Is Bound Tight)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
↗Candidate is Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessOptimality Certificate The is hamiltonian cycle witness of the linked candidate tour. ↗Lower Bound is CertifiedOptimality Certificate The is certified of the linked instance lower bound. ƒIs Same InstanceOptimality Certificate Candidate TSP instance equals lower bound TSP instance. ƒIs Bound TightOptimality Certificate Candidate travel cost equals lower bound cost.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Count of Passing Optimality Certificates Candidate Tour ƒ Scope Claim Optimality Certificate Is Optimality Proved Candidate Tour Residual Claim Candidate Tour Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 40 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ Address Instance Stop ⇢ Depot Address TSP Instance ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Optimality Certificate ⇢ TSP Instance Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ⇢ Bound Certificate TSP Bound Term ● Instance Lower Bound ID Instance Lower Bound ● Counts Toward Adjustment TSP Bound Term ● Is Certified TSP Bound Term ● Required Supplemental Term Count Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Lower Bound Optimality Certificate ● Supplemental Bound Adjustment Instance Lower Bound

Whether bound equality certifies this candidate as optimal for the declared finite instance.

ƒ Scope Claim Calculated 16 hops from ground truth string DR-148
In RuleSpeak

The optimality certificate's scope claim is determined by the following priority:

  1. “OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE” if the passing flag is set
  2. “OPTIMALITY_NOT_CERTIFIED” in all other cases
In plain words

If is passing then "OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE", otherwise "OPTIMALITY_NOT_CERTIFIED".

As a formula
= IF(Is Passing, "OPTIMAL_FOR_DECLARED_FINITE_INSTANCE", "OPTIMALITY_NOT_CERTIFIED")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs PassingOptimality Certificate Candidate is hamiltonian cycle witness, lower bound is certified, is same instance, and is bound tight.
Ground truth at the leaves 40 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ Address Instance Stop ⇢ Depot Address TSP Instance ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Optimality Certificate ⇢ TSP Instance Local Degree Bound ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Stop Local Degree Bound ⇢ First Edge Local Degree Bound ⇢ Second Edge Local Degree Bound ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Local Degree Bound Incident Dominance Check ● Local Degree Bound ID Local Degree Bound ⇢ Other Edge Incident Dominance Check ⇢ Bound Certificate TSP Bound Term ● Instance Lower Bound ID Instance Lower Bound ● Counts Toward Adjustment TSP Bound Term ● Is Certified TSP Bound Term ● Required Supplemental Term Count Instance Lower Bound ⇢ Instance Lower Bound Optimality Certificate ● Supplemental Bound Adjustment Instance Lower Bound

Honest scope of the certificate.

TSP Execution Run

Execution attempts against a concrete substrate; blocked and failed runs remain first-class evidence.

⇢ TSP Loop Relationship string

A pointer to TSP Loop. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

● Substrate Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Build Succeeded Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Whether generation succeeded.

● Database Initialized Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is Successful TSP Execution Run

Whether the database initialized.

● Conformance Succeeded Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is Successful TSP Execution Run

Whether peer conformance passed.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or BLOCKED.

● Failure Reason Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Exact blocking or failure reason.

ƒ Is Successful Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-149
In RuleSpeak

A TSP execution run is considered a successful if all of the following:

    • the build succeeded flag is set
    • the database initialized flag is set
    • the conformance succeeded flag is set
In plain words

Build succeeded, database initialized, and conformance succeeded.

As a formula
= AND(Build Succeeded, Database Initialized, Conformance Succeeded)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Build SucceededTSP Execution Run Whether generation succeeded. ●Database InitializedTSP Execution Run Whether the database initialized. ●Conformance SucceededTSP Execution Run Whether peer conformance passed.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Build Succeeded TSP Execution Run ● Database Initialized TSP Execution Run ● Conformance Succeeded TSP Execution Run

Whether all three commissioning stages succeeded.

As a formula

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 5 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is Decided TSP Edge State ƒ Commitment Rank TSP Edge State ƒ Commitment Polarity TSP Edge State ƒ Necessity Scope TSP Edge State ƒ Is Terminal Commitment TSP Edge State

UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.

● Epistemic Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Why that decision status is justified.

⇢ Inference Application Relationship string

A pointer to TSP Inference Application. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Application producing the state.

ƒ Is Decided Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-151
In RuleSpeak

A TSP edge state is considered decided if it is not the case that the decision status is “UNKNOWN”.

In plain words

Not decision status is "UNKNOWN".

As a formula
= NOT(Decision Status = "UNKNOWN")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Decision StatusTSP Edge State UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Decision Status TSP Edge State

Whether the edge is no longer unknown.

ƒ Commitment Rank Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-152
In RuleSpeak

The TSP edge state's commitment rank is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the decision status is “UNKNOWN”
  2. 1 if the decision status is “SELECTED”
  3. 2 if any of these hold:
    • the decision status is “FORCED”
    • the decision status is “FORBIDDEN”
  4. 3 in all other cases
In plain words

If decision status is "UNKNOWN" then 0, otherwise if decision status is "SELECTED" then 1, otherwise if decision status is "FORCED" or decision status is "FORBIDDEN" then 2, otherwise 3.

As a formula
= IF(Decision Status = "UNKNOWN", 0, IF(Decision Status = "SELECTED", 1, IF(OR(Decision Status = "FORCED", Decision Status = "FORBIDDEN"), 2, 3)))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Decision StatusTSP Edge State UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Decision Status TSP Edge State

Ordered strength in the edge commitment lattice.

ƒ Commitment Polarity Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-153
In RuleSpeak

The TSP edge state's commitment polarity is determined by the following priority:

  1. the negative of 1 if the decision status is “FORBIDDEN”
  2. 0 if the decision status is “UNKNOWN”
  3. 1 in all other cases
In plain words

If decision status is "FORBIDDEN" then negative 1, otherwise if decision status is "UNKNOWN" then 0, otherwise 1.

As a formula
= IF(Decision Status = "FORBIDDEN", -1, IF(Decision Status = "UNKNOWN", 0, 1))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Decision StatusTSP Edge State UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Decision Status TSP Edge State

Positive inclusion, zero unknown, or negative exclusion.

ƒ Necessity Scope Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-154
In RuleSpeak

The TSP edge state's necessity scope is determined by the following priority:

  1. “ALL_FEASIBLE_TOURS” if the decision status is “FORCED”
  2. “NO_FEASIBLE_TOUR” if the decision status is “FORBIDDEN”
  3. “CURRENT_WITNESS” if the decision status is “SELECTED”
  4. “UNRESOLVED_OR_HISTORICAL” in all other cases
In plain words

If decision status is "FORCED" then "ALL_FEASIBLE_TOURS", otherwise if decision status is "FORBIDDEN" then "NO_FEASIBLE_TOUR", otherwise if decision status is "SELECTED" then "CURRENT_WITNESS", otherwise "UNRESOLVED_OR_HISTORICAL".

As a formula
= IF(Decision Status = "FORCED", "ALL_FEASIBLE_TOURS", IF(Decision Status = "FORBIDDEN", "NO_FEASIBLE_TOUR", IF(Decision Status = "SELECTED", "CURRENT_WITNESS", "UNRESOLVED_OR_HISTORICAL")))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Decision StatusTSP Edge State UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Decision Status TSP Edge State

Scope in which the commitment holds.

ƒ Is Terminal Commitment Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-155
In RuleSpeak

A TSP edge state is considered a terminal commitment if all of the following:

    • the decision status is “FORCED”
    • the decision status is “FORBIDDEN”
    • the decision status is “CONTRADICTED”
    • the decision status is “SUPERSEDED”
In plain words

Decision status is "FORCED", decision status is "FORBIDDEN", decision status is "CONTRADICTED", or decision status is "SUPERSEDED".

As a formula
= OR(Decision Status = "FORCED", Decision Status = "FORBIDDEN", Decision Status = "CONTRADICTED", Decision Status = "SUPERSEDED")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Decision StatusTSP Edge State UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Decision Status TSP Edge State

Whether deterministic closure no longer treats the edge as open.

ƒ Is Connected Degree Two TSP Derived Edge Set

Proper cycle components.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

DERIVED_UNCERTIFIED, CONNECTED_DEGREE_TWO, DISCONNECTED_SUBTOURS, or CONTRADICTED.

ƒ Is Connected Degree Two Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-156
In RuleSpeak

A TSP derived edge set is considered a connected degree two if all of the following:

    • the degree violation count is 0
    • the connected component count is 1
    • the proper subtour count is 0
In plain words

Degree violation count equals 0, connected component count equals 1, and proper subtour count equals 0.

As a formula
= AND(Degree Violation Count = 0, Connected Component Count = 1, Proper Subtour Count = 0)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Degree Violation CountTSP Derived Edge Set Stops not having selected degree two. ●Connected Component CountTSP Derived Edge Set Connected components. ●Proper Subtour CountTSP Derived Edge Set Proper cycle components.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Degree Violation Count TSP Derived Edge Set ● Connected Component Count TSP Derived Edge Set ● Proper Subtour Count TSP Derived Edge Set

Whether this is one connected degree-two cycle.

ƒ Incidence Defect Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-157
In RuleSpeak

A TSP defect profile's incidence defect is computed as the absolute value of the required incidence minus the observed incidence.

In plain words

The absolute value of (required incidence minus observed incidence).

As a formula
= ABS(Required Incidence - Observed Incidence)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Required IncidenceTSP Defect Profile Required incident choices. ●Observed IncidenceTSP Defect Profile Observed incident choices.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one

● Component Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Connectivity Defect TSP Defect Profile Defect Vector TSP Defect Profile

Connected components represented by the subject.

ƒ Connectivity Defect Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-158
In RuleSpeak

A TSP defect profile's connectivity defect is computed as the largest of the component count minus 1 and 0.

In plain words

The largest of (component count minus 1) and 0.

As a formula
= MAX(Component Count - 1, 0)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Component CountTSP Defect Profile Connected components represented by the subject.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one

In RuleSpeak

The largest of (required boundary crossings minus observed boundary crossings) and 0.

As a formula
= MAX(Required Boundary Crossings - Observed Boundary Crossings, 0)

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

ƒ Cost Gap TSP Defect Profile Defect Vector TSP Defect Profile

● Upper Bound Cost Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Cost Gap TSP Defect Profile Defect Vector TSP Defect Profile

ƒ Cost Gap Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-160
In RuleSpeak

A TSP defect profile's cost gap is computed as the upper bound cost minus the lower bound cost.

In plain words

Upper bound cost minus lower bound cost.

As a formula
= Upper Bound Cost - Lower Bound Cost
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Upper Bound CostTSP Defect Profile Current witnessed upper value. ●Lower Bound CostTSP Defect Profile Current certified lower value.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Defect Vector TSP Defect Profile
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Upper Bound Cost TSP Defect Profile ● Lower Bound Cost TSP Defect Profile

Upper minus lower value.

ƒ Defect Vector Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-161
In RuleSpeak

A TSP defect profile's defect vector is computed as the incidence defect, followed by “|”, followed by the connectivity defect, followed by “|”, followed by the boundary defect, followed by “|”, followed by the cost gap.

In plain words

Incidence defect, "|", connectivity defect, "|", boundary defect, "|", joined with cost gap.

As a formula
= CONCAT(Incidence Defect, "|", Connectivity Defect, "|", Boundary Defect, "|", Cost Gap)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒIncidence DefectTSP Defect Profile The absolute value of (required incidence minus observed incidence). ƒConnectivity DefectTSP Defect Profile The largest of (component count minus 1) and 0. ƒBoundary DefectTSP Defect Profile The largest of (required boundary crossings minus observed boundary crossings) and 0. ƒCost GapTSP Defect Profile Upper bound cost minus lower bound cost.
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Required Incidence TSP Defect Profile ● Observed Incidence TSP Defect Profile ● Component Count TSP Defect Profile ● Required Boundary Crossings TSP Defect Profile ● Observed Boundary Crossings TSP Defect Profile ● Upper Bound Cost TSP Defect Profile ● Lower Bound Cost TSP Defect Profile

Canonical four-coordinate defect vector.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Member edges incident to the stop.

ƒ Is Degree Two Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-162
In RuleSpeak

A TSP edge set stop degree is considered a degree two if the selected degree is 2.

In plain words

Selected degree equals 2.

As a formula
= Selected Degree = 2
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Selected DegreeTSP Edge Set Stop Degree Member edges incident to the stop.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Selected Degree TSP Edge Set Stop Degree

Whether selected degree is two.
