Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is Passing TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate

Tree witness edge count.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-163
In RuleSpeak

A TSP connected degree two certificate is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the edge count is the required stop count
    • the degree violation count is 0
    • the component count is 1
    • the proper subtour count is 0
    • the spanning tree edge count is the required stop count minus 1
In plain words

Edge count equals required stop count, degree violation count equals 0, component count equals 1, proper subtour count equals 0, and spanning tree edge count equals (required stop count minus 1).

As a formula
= AND(Edge Count = Required Stop Count, Degree Violation Count = 0, Component Count = 1, Proper Subtour Count = 0, Spanning Tree Edge Count = Required Stop Count - 1)
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
●Edge CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Selected edge count. ●Required Stop CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Required stop count. ●Degree Violation CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Degree violations. ●Component CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Connected components. ●Proper Subtour CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Proper subtours. ●Spanning Tree Edge CountTSP Connected Degree Two Certificate Tree witness edge count.
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Edge Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate ● Required Stop Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate ● Degree Violation Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate ● Component Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate ● Proper Subtour Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate ● Spanning Tree Edge Count TSP Connected Degree Two Certificate

Whether the graph is one connected cycle.

ƒ Is Passing TSP Route Reconstruction

RECONSTRUCTED, NOT_EVALUABLE, or CONTRADICTED.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-164
In RuleSpeak

A TSP route reconstruction is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the reconstructed stop count is the required stop count
    • the reconstructed leg count is the required stop count
    • the candidate used as antecedent flag is not set
    • the status is “RECONSTRUCTED”
In plain words

Reconstructed stop count equals required stop count, reconstructed leg count equals required stop count, not candidate used as antecedent, and status is "RECONSTRUCTED".

As a formula
= AND(Reconstructed Stop Count = Required Stop Count, Reconstructed Leg Count = Required Stop Count, NOT(Candidate Used As Antecedent), Status = "RECONSTRUCTED")
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●Reconstructed Stop CountTSP Route Reconstruction Unique stops reconstructed. ●Required Stop CountTSP Route Reconstruction Required stops. ●Reconstructed Leg CountTSP Route Reconstruction Legs reconstructed. ●Candidate Used As AntecedentTSP Route Reconstruction Must remain false. ●StatusTSP Route Reconstruction RECONSTRUCTED, NOT_EVALUABLE, or CONTRADICTED.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Reconstructed Stop Count TSP Route Reconstruction ● Required Stop Count TSP Route Reconstruction ● Reconstructed Leg Count TSP Route Reconstruction ● Candidate Used As Antecedent TSP Route Reconstruction ● Status TSP Route Reconstruction

Whether reconstruction closes without a supplied-route antecedent.

Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Semantic Key TSP Witness Normal Form ƒ Is Valid TSP Witness Normal Form

Order defect.

ƒ Semantic Key Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-165
In RuleSpeak ⚠︎ mechanical

A TSP witness normal form's semantic key is computed as the witness shape, followed by “|”, followed by the TSP instance, followed by “|”, followed by the covered stop count, followed by “|”, followed by the required stop count, followed by “|”, followed by the edge count, followed by “|”, followed by the total cost, followed by “|”, followed by the incidence defect, followed by “|”, followed by the connectivity defect, followed by “|”, followed by the order defect, followed by “|”, followed by the boundary signature (a missing value counts as an empty string).

In plain words

Witness shape, "|", TSP instance, "|", covered stop count, "|", required stop count, "|", edge count, "|", total cost, "|", incidence defect, "|", connectivity defect, "|", order defect, "|", joined with the first of boundary signature or "" that isn't blank.

As a formula
= CONCAT(Witness Shape, "|", TSP Instance, "|", Covered Stop Count, "|", Required Stop Count, "|", Edge Count, "|", Total Cost, "|", Incidence Defect, "|", Connectivity Defect, "|", Order Defect, "|", COALESCE(Boundary Signature, ""))
Inputs 10 fields this rule reads
●Witness ShapeTSP Witness Normal Form CYCLE or PATH. ⇢TSP InstanceTSP Witness Normal Form Owning instance. ●Covered Stop CountTSP Witness Normal Form Covered distinct stops. ●Required Stop CountTSP Witness Normal Form Required stops in witness scope. ●Edge CountTSP Witness Normal Form Witness edge count. ●Total CostTSP Witness Normal Form Witness cost. ●Incidence DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Incidence defect. ●Connectivity DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Connectivity defect. ●Order DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Order defect. ●Boundary SignatureTSP Witness Normal Form Scope/entry/exit signature for path witnesses.
Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Witness Shape TSP Witness Normal Form ⇢ TSP Instance TSP Witness Normal Form ● Covered Stop Count TSP Witness Normal Form ● Required Stop Count TSP Witness Normal Form ● Edge Count TSP Witness Normal Form ● Total Cost TSP Witness Normal Form ● Incidence Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Connectivity Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Order Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Boundary Signature TSP Witness Normal Form

Provenance-independent witness signature.

ƒ Is Valid Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-166
In RuleSpeak

A TSP witness normal form is considered valid if all of the following:

    • the covered stop count is the required stop count
    • the incidence defect is 0
    • the connectivity defect is 0
    • the order defect is 0
    • any of these hold:
      • all of these hold:
        • the witness shape is “CYCLE”
        • the edge count is the required stop count
      • all of these hold:
        • the witness shape is “PATH”
        • the edge count is the required stop count minus 1
In plain words

Covered stop count equals required stop count, incidence defect equals 0, connectivity defect equals 0, order defect equals 0, and witness shape is "CYCLE" and edge count equals required stop count or witness shape is "PATH" and edge count equals (required stop count minus 1).

As a formula
= AND(Covered Stop Count = Required Stop Count, Incidence Defect = 0, Connectivity Defect = 0, Order Defect = 0, OR(AND(Witness Shape = "CYCLE", Edge Count = Required Stop Count), AND(Witness Shape = "PATH", Edge Count = Required Stop Count - 1)))
Inputs 7 fields this rule reads
●Covered Stop CountTSP Witness Normal Form Covered distinct stops. ●Required Stop CountTSP Witness Normal Form Required stops in witness scope. ●Incidence DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Incidence defect. ●Connectivity DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Connectivity defect. ●Order DefectTSP Witness Normal Form Order defect. ●Witness ShapeTSP Witness Normal Form CYCLE or PATH. ●Edge CountTSP Witness Normal Form Witness edge count.
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Covered Stop Count TSP Witness Normal Form ● Required Stop Count TSP Witness Normal Form ● Incidence Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Connectivity Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Order Defect TSP Witness Normal Form ● Witness Shape TSP Witness Normal Form ● Edge Count TSP Witness Normal Form

Whether the normal form is a valid cycle or path.

● Provenance Summary Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Branching Avoided Pct Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Percentage of branch search avoided.

ƒ Route Class Elimination Pct Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-167
In RuleSpeak

The TSP search certificate's route class elimination pct is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the initial route class count is 0
  2. the initial route class count minus the surviving route class count divided by the initial route class count times 100 rounded to 2 decimal place(s) in all other cases
In plain words

If initial route class count equals 0 then 0, otherwise (((initial route class count minus surviving route class count) divided by initial route class count) times 100) rounded to 2 decimal places.

As a formula
= IF(Initial Route Class Count = 0, 0, ROUND((Initial Route Class Count - Surviving Route Class Count) / Initial Route Class Count * 100, 2))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Initial Route Class CountTSP Search Certificate Symmetry-reduced classes initially possible. ●Surviving Route Class CountTSP Search Certificate Classes after deterministic closure.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Initial Route Class Count TSP Search Certificate ● Surviving Route Class Count TSP Search Certificate

Percentage of route classes removed.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

CERTIFIED or RESIDUAL_SEARCH.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

SURVIVES or DOMINATED.

ƒ Boundary Signature Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-168
In RuleSpeak

A TSP cluster boundary state's boundary signature is computed as the neighborhood, followed by “|”, followed by the entry stop, followed by “|”, followed by the exit stop, followed by “|”, followed by the internal stop count, followed by “|”, followed by the internal path cost.

In plain words

Neighborhood, "|", entry stop, "|", exit stop, "|", internal stop count, "|", joined with internal path cost.

As a formula
= CONCAT(Neighborhood, "|", Entry Stop, "|", Exit Stop, "|", Internal Stop Count, "|", Internal Path Cost)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
⇢NeighborhoodTSP Cluster Boundary State Contracted neighborhood. ⇢Entry StopTSP Cluster Boundary State Canonical first boundary stop. ⇢Exit StopTSP Cluster Boundary State Canonical second boundary stop. ●Internal Stop CountTSP Cluster Boundary State Stops covered inside the cluster. ●Internal Path CostTSP Cluster Boundary State Certified internal path cost.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Neighborhood TSP Cluster Boundary State ⇢ Entry Stop TSP Cluster Boundary State ⇢ Exit Stop TSP Cluster Boundary State ● Internal Stop Count TSP Cluster Boundary State ● Internal Path Cost TSP Cluster Boundary State

Scope/entry/exit/coverage/cost signature.

ƒ Semantic Quotient Key Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-169
In RuleSpeak

A TSP cluster boundary state's semantic quotient key is computed as the neighborhood, followed by “|”, followed by the entry stop, followed by “|”, followed by the exit stop, followed by “|”, followed by the internal stop count, followed by “|”, followed by the internal path cost.

In plain words

Neighborhood, "|", entry stop, "|", exit stop, "|", internal stop count, "|", joined with internal path cost.

As a formula
= CONCAT(Neighborhood, "|", Entry Stop, "|", Exit Stop, "|", Internal Stop Count, "|", Internal Path Cost)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
⇢NeighborhoodTSP Cluster Boundary State Contracted neighborhood. ⇢Entry StopTSP Cluster Boundary State Canonical first boundary stop. ⇢Exit StopTSP Cluster Boundary State Canonical second boundary stop. ●Internal Stop CountTSP Cluster Boundary State Stops covered inside the cluster. ●Internal Path CostTSP Cluster Boundary State Certified internal path cost.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Neighborhood TSP Cluster Boundary State ⇢ Entry Stop TSP Cluster Boundary State ⇢ Exit Stop TSP Cluster Boundary State ● Internal Stop Count TSP Cluster Boundary State ● Internal Path Cost TSP Cluster Boundary State

Equivalence key preserving boundary signature and cost.

● Orientation Multiplicity Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Raw directed orientations represented by the undirected state.

● Is Quotient Representative Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Whether this state is the canonical quotient representative.

A pointer to Instance Stop. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Dominance Delta Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

ƒ Is Fiber Minimum Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-170
In RuleSpeak

A TSP cluster boundary state is considered a fiber minimum if all of the following:

    • the quotient representative flag is set
    • the dominated flag is not set
In plain words

Is quotient representative and not is dominated.

As a formula
= AND(Is Quotient Representative, NOT(Is Dominated))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Is Quotient RepresentativeTSP Cluster Boundary State Whether this state is the canonical quotient representative. ●Is DominatedTSP Cluster Boundary State Whether a cheaper state with the same boundary exists.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Quotient Representative TSP Cluster Boundary State ● Is Dominated TSP Cluster Boundary State

Whether the state is the surviving minimum in its fiber.

ƒ Semantic Compression Pct TSP Convergence Measurement

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Semantic Compression Pct TSP Convergence Measurement

Active primitive count after reduction.

● New Primitive Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

New primitives introduced by this loop.

● Derived Alias Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Named derived concepts retained above the basis.

● Physical Table Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Canonical physical table count at this loop.

ƒ Semantic Compression Pct Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-176
In RuleSpeak

The TSP convergence measurement's semantic compression pct is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the surface concept count before is 0
  2. the surface concept count before minus the primitive count after divided by the surface concept count before times 100 rounded to 2 decimal place(s) in all other cases
In plain words

If surface concept count before equals 0 then 0, otherwise (((surface concept count before minus primitive count after) divided by surface concept count before) times 100) rounded to 2 decimal places.

As a formula
= IF(Surface Concept Count Before = 0, 0, ROUND((Surface Concept Count Before - Primitive Count After) / Surface Concept Count Before * 100, 2))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Surface Concept Count BeforeTSP Convergence Measurement Surface vocabulary count before quotienting. ●Primitive Count AfterTSP Convergence Measurement Active primitive count after reduction.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Surface Concept Count Before TSP Convergence Measurement ● Primitive Count After TSP Convergence Measurement

Surface-to-basis reduction percentage.

● Novel Term Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Predicate coined or stabilized by the loop.

● Prediction Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

NOT_TESTED, EARLY_SUPPORT, COUNTEREVIDENCE, or SUPPORTED_FOR_CURRENT_DOMAIN.

● Notes Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Scope and interpretation.

ƒ Is Current Basis Member Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-177
In RuleSpeak

A TSP concept registry is considered a current basis member if all of the following:

    • the status is “ACTIVE_PRIMITIVE”
    • the status is “ACTIVE_OPERATOR”
In plain words

Status is "ACTIVE_PRIMITIVE" or status is "ACTIVE_OPERATOR".

As a formula
= OR(Status = "ACTIVE_PRIMITIVE", Status = "ACTIVE_OPERATOR")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●StatusTSP Concept Registry ACTIVE_PRIMITIVE, ACTIVE_DERIVED, or HISTORICAL_PROJECTION.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Status TSP Concept Registry

Whether the row is an active atom or operator.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Search Elimination Pct Search Metric

● Backtrack Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Residual Ambiguity Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Remaining unresolved route classes or boundary states.

ƒ Search Elimination Pct Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-178
In RuleSpeak

The search metric's search elimination pct is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the branch count before is 0
  2. the branch count before minus the branch count after divided by the branch count before times 100 rounded to 2 decimal place(s) in all other cases
In plain words

If branch count before equals 0 then 0, otherwise (((branch count before minus branch count after) divided by branch count before) times 100) rounded to 2 decimal places.

As a formula
= IF(Branch Count Before = 0, 0, ROUND((Branch Count Before - Branch Count After) / Branch Count Before * 100, 2))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Branch Count BeforeSearch Metric Branches before the current inference stage. ●Branch Count AfterSearch Metric Branches after the current inference stage.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Branch Count Before Search Metric ● Branch Count After Search Metric

Percentage of branches removed without search.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Interpretation of the metric row.

↗ Actual Hamiltonian Cycle Witness TSP Invariant Check ↗ Actual Optimality Proved TSP Invariant Check ↗ Actual Total Travel Cost TSP Invariant Check Is Hamiltonian Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Optimality Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Cost Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

↗ Actual Hamiltonian Cycle Witness Lookup 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-180
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check's actual hamiltonian cycle witness is true when the TSP invariant check's candidate tour is a hamiltonian cycle witness.

In plain words

The is hamiltonian cycle witness of the linked candidate tour.

As a formula
= INDEX(Is Hamiltonian Cycle Witness, MATCH(Candidate Tour, Candidate Tour ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessCandidate Tour Count of tour stops equals required stop count, count of tour legs equals required stop count, count of non unique visits equals 0, count of non unique sequences equals 0, count of out of instance stops equals 0, count of non required stops equals 0, count of depot stops equals 1, count of depot position violations equals 0, count of invalid legs equals 0, count of cycle degree violations equals 0, and count of non unique leg orders equals 0. ⇢Candidate TourTSP Invariant Check Candidate tour under test. ●Candidate Tour IDCandidate Tour Stable candidate-tour identifier.
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Hamiltonian Status Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 24 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ Address Instance Stop ⇢ Depot Address TSP Instance ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour TSP Invariant Check

In RuleSpeak

In plain words

The is optimality proved of the linked candidate tour.

As a formula
= INDEX(Is Optimality Proved, MATCH(Candidate Tour, Candidate Tour ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Optimality ProvedCandidate Tour Is hamiltonian cycle witness and count of passing optimality certificates is greater than 0. ⇢Candidate TourTSP Invariant Check Candidate tour under test. ●Candidate Tour IDCandidate Tour Stable candidate-tour identifier.

● Expected Total Travel Cost Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

ƒ Is Cost Correct TSP Invariant Check Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

Expected sum of represented valid leg costs.

↗ Actual Total Travel Cost Lookup 8 hops from ground truth number DR-182
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check's actual total travel cost — taken from the linked candidate tour.

In plain words

The total travel cost of the linked candidate tour.

As a formula
= INDEX(Total Travel Cost, MATCH(Candidate Tour, Candidate Tour ID, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Travel CostCandidate Tour Sum of travel cost across all tour legs whose candidate tour is candidate tour ID. ⇢Candidate TourTSP Invariant Check Candidate tour under test. ●Candidate Tour IDCandidate Tour Stable candidate-tour identifier.

ƒ Is Hamiltonian Status Correct Calculated 9 hops from ground truth boolean DR-183
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check is considered a hamiltonian status correct if the expected hamiltonian cycle witness is the actual hamiltonian cycle witness.

In plain words

Expected hamiltonian cycle witness equals actual hamiltonian cycle witness.

As a formula
= Expected Hamiltonian Cycle Witness = Actual Hamiltonian Cycle Witness
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Expected Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessTSP Invariant Check Expected derived route-validity result. ↗Actual Hamiltonian Cycle WitnessTSP Invariant Check The is hamiltonian cycle witness of the linked candidate tour.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Is Passing TSP Invariant Check

● Expected Hamiltonian Cycle Witness TSP Invariant Check ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ⇢ Address Instance Stop ⇢ Depot Address TSP Instance ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour TSP Invariant Check

ƒ Is Optimality Status Correct Calculated 19 hops from ground truth boolean DR-184
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check is considered an optimality status correct if the expected optimality proved is the actual optimality proved.

In plain words

Expected optimality proved equals actual optimality proved.

As a formula
= Expected Optimality Proved = Actual Optimality Proved
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Expected Optimality ProvedTSP Invariant Check Expected optimality-certificate status. ↗Actual Optimality ProvedTSP Invariant Check The is optimality proved of the linked candidate tour.

ƒ Is Cost Correct Calculated 9 hops from ground truth boolean DR-185
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check is considered a cost correct if the expected total travel cost is the actual total travel cost.

In plain words

Expected total travel cost equals actual total travel cost.

As a formula
= Expected Total Travel Cost = Actual Total Travel Cost
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Expected Total Travel CostTSP Invariant Check Expected sum of represented valid leg costs. ↗Actual Total Travel CostTSP Invariant Check The total travel cost of the linked candidate tour.

Ground truth at the leaves 24 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expected Total Travel Cost TSP Invariant Check ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Leg ⇢ Candidate Tour Tour Stop ⇢ From Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Tour Stop ID Tour Stop ⇢ To Tour Stop Tour Leg ● Sequence Position Tour Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Instance Stop ● TSP Instance ID TSP Instance ● Is Required Instance Stop ⇢ TSP Instance Candidate Tour ● Candidate Tour ID Candidate Tour ⇢ Instance Stop Tour Stop ⇢ From Stop Travel Edge ⇢ Travel Edge Tour Leg ● Travel Edge ID Travel Edge ⇢ To Stop Travel Edge ⇢ TSP Instance Travel Edge ● Is Available Travel Edge ● Instance Stop ID Instance Stop ● Canonical Pair Key Travel Edge ● Leg Order Tour Leg ● Travel Cost Travel Edge ⇢ Candidate Tour TSP Invariant Check

Whether expected and actual total cost agree.

ƒ Is Passing Calculated 20 hops from ground truth boolean DR-186
In RuleSpeak

A TSP invariant check is considered passing if all of the following:

    • the hamiltonian status correct flag is set
    • the optimality status correct flag is set
    • the cost correct flag is set
In plain words

Is hamiltonian status correct, is optimality status correct, and is cost correct.

As a formula
= AND(Is Hamiltonian Status Correct, Is Optimality Status Correct, Is Cost Correct)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Hamiltonian Status CorrectTSP Invariant Check Expected hamiltonian cycle witness equals actual hamiltonian cycle witness. ƒIs Optimality Status CorrectTSP Invariant Check Expected optimality proved equals actual optimality proved. ƒIs Cost CorrectTSP Invariant Check Expected total travel cost equals actual total travel cost.
